mnemonics.title: Five Components of FA
mnemonics.content: Q(ueen) S(ays) T(o) F(ollow) G(reat) - Q=States, S=Start, T=Transition, F=Final, G=Alphabet

mnemonics.title: DFA vs NFA
mnemonics.content: D-Determined: One path per input | N-Not determined: Multiple paths possible

acronyms.usage: Mathematical model with finite states for language recognition
FA where each state-symbol pair gives exactly one next state
FA where each state-symbol pair can give multiple next states or none
Set of all valid input symbols for the FA
Defines how state changes based on input symbol
String of length zero; used in ε-transitions of NFA

visualTips.visualization: Draw states as circles. Use arrow with 'start' label pointing to initial state. Double circles for final states. Directed edges (arrows) labeled with input symbols connect states.
Rows = states (including start), Columns = input symbols. Fill each cell with the next state. Empty cells = undefined transitions.
DFA: One arrow per symbol from each circle. NFA: Multiple arrows per symbol OR missing arrows OR ε-arrows.

analogies.analogy: Think of a vending machine - it has finite states (waiting for coin, selecting item, dispensing), reads input (coins, buttons), and either completes transaction (accepts) or gets stuck (rejects).
Like a traffic light controller - current state + input (sensor) determines next state (light color change).
DFA is like a single-path hallway with signs at each door. NFA is like multiple possible paths through a maze - at any point, you could take any available door.
